meta.notes: Single source of truth for all SecureCode v2.0 statistics. This file documents both paper claims and actual dataset values, with discrepancies flagged for correction.

v1_internal_subset.description: Earlier compliance validation subset referenced in paper Sections 1.3, 4.2, 5.1
v1_internal_subset.notes: This was an earlier version of the dataset. The paper inconsistently references both v1 (841 train) and v2 (1934 train) numbers. All references to 841 should be updated to reflect the final 1934 training examples.

canonical.split_method: NOT stratified by CVE - CVE overlap exists between splits (see split_leakage_analysis)
canonical.stratification_notes: Distribution varies somewhat across splits, indicating imperfect stratification. See distribution_per_split section.

split_leakage_analysis.leakage_status: WARNING: Significant CVE overlap exists between splits
split_leakage_analysis.recommendation: For proper evaluation, examples sharing the same CVE should be grouped in the same split to prevent information leakage. Current splits may overestimate model performance.

owasp_category_distribution._note: Paper claims do not match actual data. Using ACTUAL values as canonical.

language_distribution._note: Language distribution matches paper claims exactly.

severity_distribution._note: Minor discrepancy: Paper claims off by 1 for CRITICAL and HIGH.

distribution_per_split.stratification_assessment: Moderate variation exists across splits. Val set has higher CRITICAL percentage (72.0%) vs train (64.2%). Distribution is approximately stratified but not perfectly balanced.

discrepancies_found.description: Paper references outdated dataset size (841 train) in multiple sections while also claiming 1934 train
discrepancies_found.resolution: Update all references from 841 to 1934 for training set

discrepancies_found.description: OWASP category distribution in paper does not match actual data
discrepancies_found.resolution: Update all OWASP percentages and counts to match actual dataset values

discrepancies_found.description: Severity distribution off by 1 example (CRITICAL: 1582 vs 1581, HIGH: 788 vs 789)
discrepancies_found.resolution: Update CRITICAL from 1581 to 1582, HIGH from 789 to 788

discrepancies_found.description: IRR sample size conflict (200 vs 50 examples)
discrepancies_found.resolution: Determine actual sample size and make consistent throughout

discrepancies_found.description: CVE overlap between train/val/test splits constitutes data leakage
discrepancies_found.details: 111 CVEs overlap train-val, 106 CVEs overlap train-test
discrepancies_found.resolution: Either re-split data to prevent CVE overlap, or document this limitation explicitly

discrepancies_found.description: Older dataset split sizes in paper (train: 841, val: 175, test: 193 = 1209 total)
discrepancies_found.resolution: Update compliance journey tables to reflect current dataset size or clarify these were earlier validation phases

human_decisions_required.question: Should the OWASP distribution claims in the paper be updated to match actual data, or does the data need to be re-categorized?
human_decisions_required.impact: High - affects multiple sections and appendices
human_decisions_required.recommendation: Update paper to match actual data since the dataset files represent ground truth

human_decisions_required.question: What is the correct IRR sample size - 200 or 50 examples?
human_decisions_required.recommendation: Verify with actual review records and use single consistent number

human_decisions_required.question: How should the compliance journey section be updated given the dataset grew from 841 to 1934 training examples?
options: A) Reframe as historical validation on earlier subset, then document expansion
B) Re-run validation on full dataset and update all numbers
C) Keep historical numbers but add clear context explaining this was v1 validation
human_decisions_required.recommendation: Option C - preserve historical accuracy while clarifying context

human_decisions_required.question: Should the dataset be re-split to prevent CVE leakage, or should this be documented as a known limitation?
human_decisions_required.impact: High - affects validity of evaluation claims
human_decisions_required.recommendation: For a production-grade dataset, recommend re-splitting with CVE grouping
